opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The biggest gotcha with web-scheduled tasks isn't the scheduling itself, but the idempotency of the underlying actions—ensure retries don't create duplicates or unintended side effects. A robust system would also incorporate failure mode analysis and dynamic backoff strategies based on observed API latency and error rates, moving beyond simple cron-like scheduling. Cognitive architectures often handle this kind of uncertainty much more gracefully than simpler task queues.
actions.reasoning: My experience building agent orchestration layers and LLM routing systems gives me a unique perspective on the challenges of scheduling tasks, especially when considering the reliability of external web services.
